methodology: CDC ADDM Network active surveillance 11 sites 2020
notes: CDC ADDM 2020 surveillance — autism prevalence 1 in 36 (~2.78%) age 8. Used as engine general-population base rate when no family history.

methodology: systematic review of global prevalence studies
notes: Zeidan 2022 Autism Res — global prevalence systematic review update. Widely-cited median ~1% globally; substantial heterogeneity by region.

methodology: Swedish nationwide registry full-sibling recurrence
notes: Sandin 2014 JAMA — Swedish nationwide registry. Full-sibling recurrence ~13%. Engine uses this as base rate when family has ≥1 autistic sibling. Also reports paternal age effects.

methodology: Swedish nationwide twin + sibling heritability analysis
notes: Sandin 2017 JAMA — autism heritability estimate ~83% (95% CI 0.50-0.87 in twins). Genetic component dominates; environment substantial in residual.

methodology: Israeli draft-board cohort population study
notes: Reichenberg 2006 Arch Gen Psychiatry — paternal age >40 RR ~5.75 vs <30 in this cohort. Engine adjusts maternal/paternal-age priors per this finding.

methodology: serum FRAA testing in autism cohort
notes: Frye 2013 Mol Psychiatry — FRAA blocking and/or binding antibody positive in ~75% of autism cohort. Subsequent cohorts vary 30-75%; uses FRAA positive as PHE-0001 marker.

methodology: clinical recognition + CSF 5-MTHF + FRAA testing
notes: Ramaekers 2013 Clin Chem Lab Med — clinical CFD spectrum review. Strict CFD with low CSF 5-MTHF much lower prevalence than FRAA-positive subset; treatable with leucovorin.

methodology: 12-week placebo-controlled RCT
notes: Frye 2018 Mol Psychiatry — RCT leucovorin in FRAA+ autism with language impairment; substantial improvement in verbal communication. Calibration anchor INT-0001 supporting evidence.

methodology: systematic review and meta-analysis of mito biomarkers
notes: Rossignol 2012 Mol Psychiatry — meta-analysis. ~30% show mito biomarker elevation; ~5% meet strict mito disease criteria. Wide range reflects test sensitivity/specificity variation.

methodology: systematic review of mito biomarkers in ASD
notes: Frye 2024 Neurobiol Dis — broader systematic review. When any mito biomarker abnormal counted, prevalence rises substantially.

methodology: GI mucosal biopsy mitochondrial function blinded
notes: Rose 2017 PLoS One — mito dysfunction in GI mucosa of autistic children. Cross-link between PHE-0002 and PHE-0004 — mito + GI cluster.

methodology: comprehensive review of autism regression literature
notes: Stefanatos 2008 Neuropsychol Rev — regression in ASD review. ~25-40% of autism cohorts report developmental regression; methodology variation contributes to range.

methodology: home video validation of parent-reported regression
notes: Werner 2005 Arch Gen Psychiatry — validation of autism regression using home video. Confirms parent-reported regression is identifiable on prospective video review; not solely recall artifact.

methodology: consensus expert review of GI in ASD
notes: Buie 2010 Pediatrics — consensus on GI evaluation in ASD. Wide reported prevalence range (20-85%) reflects diagnostic-criterion variation. Engine uses ~50% as point estimate.

methodology: cross-sectional comparison autism vs typical-developing
notes: Adams 2011 BMC Gastroenterol — GI flora and GI status differs systematically autism vs typical-developing controls.

methodology: TOSCA international natural history TSC cohort
notes: de Vries 2018 Orphanet — TOSCA international TSC natural history cohort. ~40% TSC patients have autism features; range 20-60% across age groups.

methodology: estimated from TSC + PTEN + other mTOR-pathway syndromic combined
notes: de Vries 2020 J Neurodev Disord — TAND clusters. PHE-0005 mTOR-syndromic autism is ~5-15% of full autism population (TSC ~1-3% + PTEN-PHTS ~1-2% + other rare mTOR syndromes ~0-5%). Confidence LOW reflects rough additive estimate.

methodology: ADI-R + ADOS in FXS male cohort
notes: Harris 2008 Am J Ment Retard — autism profile in FXS males ~50%. Distinct from idiopathic autism profile but meets diagnostic criteria.

methodology: FMR1 testing in autism cohorts
notes: Harris 2008 + general FXS literature — FXS is ~1-6% of autism cases (lower bound = strict criteria; upper bound includes premutations).

methodology: 3-month placebo-controlled bumetanide RCT
notes: Lemonnier 2012 Transl Psychiatry — bumetanide RCT in autism. Subset shows clinically-meaningful improvement; defines PHE-0007 GABA/Cl⁻ phenotype. Phase 3 trials showed mixed results consistent with effect heterogeneity (per CLAUDE.md §9).

methodology: review of GABA developmental sequence + autism literature
notes: Ben-Ari 2014 Neuroscience — GABA developmental shift framework. Mechanistic estimate of subset with persistent immature GABA polarity; LOW confidence reflects mechanistic-only basis.

methodology: extrapolated from Walsh framework population claims; NO primary PMID available — Rossignol 2012 cited as proximal methylation-cycle evidence in autism
notes: Walsh framework claims ~30-40% undermethylator in autism. No published primary RCT supports this directly. Rows cite proximal methylation-cycle dysfunction evidence (James 2004 / Rossignol meta) rather than fabricating Walsh-specific PMID. Confidence LOW per audit H-10 framework.

methodology: extrapolated from Walsh framework; no primary PMID available
notes: Walsh framework claims ~5-10% overmethylator in autism. Same caveats as PHE-0008. Confidence LOW.

methodology: extrapolated from Walsh framework; no primary PMID available
notes: Walsh framework claims ~10-20% pyroluria in autism. Same caveats. Confidence LOW.

methodology: extrapolated from Walsh framework; no primary PMID available
notes: Walsh framework claims ~15-25% Cu:Zn imbalance in autism. Same caveats. Confidence LOW.

methodology: chromosomal microarray + whole-exome sequencing prospective autism cohort
notes: Tammimies 2015 JAMA — molecular diagnostic yield ~16% (CMA + WES) in autism. Sets engine prior on probability that a syndromic-genetic cause can be identified.

methodology: clinical and genomic evaluation of PMS cohort
notes: Sarasua 2014 Hum Genet — 201 PMS patients clinical/genomic evaluation. ~84% meet autism criteria within PMS. Cross-ref RSG-0006.

methodology: Swedish nationwide population-based study with meta-analytic comparison
notes: [H-11 audit fix] Hultman 2011 Mol Psychiatry (PMID 21116277) — Swedish nationwide cohort + meta-analysis of paternal age and autism risk. RR per decade ~1.3-1.5 in meta-analyzed studies. SUPERSEDES single-study estimate in BPP-0005 (Reichenberg 2006 reported RR 5.75 for >40 vs <30 in Israeli draft-board cohort; high-end of single-study range and not representative of the meta-analytic estimate). Engine should prefer this row over BPP-0005 when computing paternal age modifier.

methodology: 5-country population-based cohort
notes: [H-11 audit fix] Sandin 2016 Mol Psychiatry (PMID 26055426) — 5.7M-person 5-country cohort. Both maternal and paternal age contribute; effect of age difference between parents also significant. Engine modifier: combined parental-age effect rather than paternal age alone. Both Hultman 2011 and Sandin 2016 support meta-analytic per-decade RR ~1.2-1.5 — much smaller than Reichenberg 2006 single-study RR 5.75.
